endpointFredholmObstructionFormulaStatus.label: endpoint Fredholm obstruction formula
endpointFredholmObstructionFormulaStatus.reason: The exact endpoint problem is Mz=-b(d); compatibility is w^*b(d)=0 for every w in ker M^*.

greenPairingFormulaForObstructionStatus.label: Green pairing formula for obstruction
greenPairingFormulaForObstructionStatus.reason: Green's identity and the exact jump law give w^*b(d)=int K_d P f_w - d(f_w) for the primal endpoint test f_w.

finiteSampledObstructionAnnihilationStat.label: finite sampled near-obstruction annihilation
finiteSampledObstructionAnnihilationStat.reason: The resolved relative-threshold left endpoint direction must annihilate the actual source endpoint vectors.  This is stronger than solving the finite endpoint equation with the full sampled matrix.

fullEndpointRowRankVacuityStatus.label: full endpoint row rank vacuity
fullEndpointRowRankVacuityStatus.reason: If the rank-ball certificate is rigorous and sigma_min(M)>0, then ker(M^*)={0}; there is no Fredholm obstruction to annihilate.

volterraSturmPrimalObstructionAnnihilati.label: Volterra/Sturm primal obstruction annihilation
volterraSturmPrimalObstructionAnnihilati.reason: Still need the non-circular continuum theorem: every primal homogeneous endpoint obstruction f_w annihilates the actual active source rows d_u.

continuumEndpointObstructionCompatibilit.label: continuum endpoint obstruction compatibility
continuumEndpointObstructionCompatibilit.reason: Closed either by rigorous full active row rank of M, which makes ker(M^*) trivial, or by combining the Green pairing formula with the Volterra/Sturm primal obstruction annihilation theorem.

actualEndpointRhsInRange.label: actual endpoint RHS in Range(M)
actualEndpointRhsInRange.reason: For the actual source rows, the endpoint vectors b(d) are in the finite endpoint-map range.

activeEndpointKilled.label: active endpoint concomitant killed
activeEndpointKilled.reason: Minimum-norm z solves Mz=-b(d) on the active two-dimensional source space.

finiteLeftObstructionAnnihilatesActualRo.label: finite left obstruction annihilates actual rows
finiteLeftObstructionAnnihilatesActualRo.reason: The resolved left endpoint obstruction annihilates the actual source endpoint vectors.

finiteEvidence.interpretation: Finite sampled-flow endpoint evidence.  A tiny full solve residual only proves the sampled endpoint equation can be solved; it does not by itself prove annihilation of continuum Fredholm obstructions.

conditionalBallFullRowRankStatus.label: conditional ball full active row rank
conditionalBallFullRowRankStatus.reason: Weyl perturbation gives sigma_min(M_exact)>=sigma_min(M0)-delta for the supplied endpoint-map ball.

rigorousEndpointMapFullRowRankStatus.label: rigorous endpoint map full active row rank
rigorousEndpointMapFullRowRankStatus.reason: Closed only if the supplied endpoint-map ball is a rigorous interval enclosure of the exact fundamental-matrix endpoint map.

kernelMStarZeroStatus.label: ker M^* is zero
kernelMStarZeroStatus.reason: If the rigorous full-row-rank status is closed, then the left Fredholm obstruction space is trivial and endpoint compatibility is vacuous.

rankBallEvidence.remainingAnalyticGap: Produce a rigorous interval enclosure for the exact endpoint fundamental-matrix map with Frobenius radius below the displayed maxAllowedFrobeniusRadiusForRank.

sampledCenterStabilityStatus.label: sampled finite-difference endpoint center stability
sampledCenterStabilityStatus.reason: Stable only if mesh-refinement endpoint maps remain within a small constant factor.  They currently vary by many orders of magnitude.

exactSegmentCenterStabilityStatus.label: exact-segment derivative endpoint center stability
exactSegmentCenterStabilityStatus.reason: Stable only if exact-derivative segment refinements remain within a small constant factor.  Current piecewise-constant propagation still varies by many orders of magnitude.

rankBallCenterReadyStatus.label: rank-ball center ready
rankBallCenterReadyStatus.reason: A rank-ball proof needs a stable center before interval radii can be meaningful.  Current centers are not stable enough.

centerStabilityEvidence.conclusion: The desired endpoint-map rank proof cannot be closed from the current sampled-flow center.  The next necessary artifact is a validated ODE construction for the adjoint companion system, preferably using analytic/confluent trace derivatives and a Taylor or Chebyshev model for A(s) rather than piecewise-constant mesh propagation.
centerStabilityEvidence.nextTarget: Build a stable validated endpoint-flow solver that encloses the exact fundamental matrix Phi(a,s0) and endpoint rows with Frobenius endpoint-map radius below the rank margin.

proof: For the exact fundamental matrix endpoint map, the endpoint equation is Mz=-b(d).  The finite-dimensional Fredholm alternative gives compatibility iff ker(M^*) annihilates b(d).
Let w in ker(M^*) and let f_w be the corresponding primal endpoint test.  The condition w^*M=0 says all homogeneous adjoint endpoint concomitants vanish against f_w.
For the jump Green coefficient K_d, integrate dB_P[K_d,f_w]=K_d P f_w-f_w P^*K_d across I.  The jump law turns P^*K_d into the source row d.
Thus w^*b(d)=int_I K_d(a)P f_w(a)da-d(f_w).  If f_w is a primal homogeneous endpoint obstruction, this reduces to w^*b(d)=-d(f_w).
The remaining continuum theorem is therefore d_u(f_w)=0 for the actual active source family and every primal homogeneous endpoint obstruction f_w.
Alternatively, a rigorous rank-ball proof that sigma_min(M)>0 makes ker(M^*) trivial and closes endpoint compatibility without an obstruction-annihilation theorem.
remainingAnalyticGap: Build a stable validated endpoint-flow solver that encloses the exact fundamental matrix Phi(a,s0) and endpoint rows with Frobenius endpoint-map radius below the rank margin.
